user_message: I have this meeting transcript from Iron Creek Mining that needs draft the follow-up. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-03-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Kevin (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:18:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:20:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:25:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:29:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:34:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:38:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: MSX updates warranted from meeting outcomes

required_artifacts: action items with owners and due dates

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF12-E trap (Follow-up email tone doesn't match meeting register); produces complete Decision log, action items with owners and due dates, customer-facing follow-up email draft, MSX wri
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF12-E failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF12-E failure mode (Follow-up email tone doesn't match meeting register)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF12-E -- Follow-up email tone doesn't match meeting register
Write proposals must require explicit user approval
